item.title: NYT Strands #737 Hints and Answers for Today, March 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #737 for Tuesday, March 10, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 10, 2026

The Strands’ answers for Game #737 are all about different ways to say hello. So, brush up on your greetings before you get your guesses ready

General Strands Hints For March 10

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Mr. Hanky loves this greeting.
  • Hint 2: Sailors love this phrase, matey.
  • Hint 3: This is how AOL greeted people before telling them they had mail.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #737 for March 10, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 10, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • HOWL
  • MODEST
  • WOOD
  • ANTE
  • COME

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #737 On March 10, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • WELCOME
  • HELLO
  • HOWDY
  • GREETINGS
  • SALUTATIONS
  • AHOY
  • SPANAGRAM: GOODDAY

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #737?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 11 will be Strands Game #738.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: What Time Does Rocket League Season 22 Start?
item.content:encoded:
Rocket League Season 22 is not far away now, as the latest update from Epic Games for the “soccar” game releases on March 11 and will directly impact the game’s esports ecosystem before the upcoming Paris Major.

The new season begins just in time for Split 2 of the Rocket League Championship Series (RLCS) 2026 season, which kicks off with regional esports tournaments later this month following Gentle Mates’ triumph at the Boston Major last week.

And Season 22 will allow Rocket League fans to impact the upcoming Paris Major with the game’s first-ever Bracket Rivals, an in-game event that will decide the match-up for a content creator showmatch at the LAN event.

Between March 20 and 26, Rocket League players will be able to pick up free titles representing the community’s favourite creator duos. The most goals scored by players donning each title will then decide which two influencer teams will get to play on stage in Paris come May.

As well as this new event, the update is also bringing various quality-of-life upgrades to the 10-year-old game. Now, Rocket League players will be able to see their matchmaking rating (MMR) for the first time.

Additionally, the recharge progress for boost pads will now be visible to players, and an indicator to show when your flip reset is off cooldown is being added to the game, too.

Rocket League Season 22 Global Release Times

The exact date and release time for the Rocket League Season 22 update in every major region is as follows:

Pacific Time (PT) – 9:00 March 11
Mountain Time (MT) – 10:00 March 11
Central Time (CT) – 11:00 March 11
Eastern Time (ET) – 12:00 March 11
Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC) – 17:00 March 11
Eastern European Time (EET) – 19:00 March 11
Moscow Standard Time (MSK) – 20:00 March 11
Indian Standard Time (IST) – 21:30 March 11
China Standard Time (CST) – 1:00 March 12, 2026, at 1 am
Japan Standard Time (JST) – 2:00 March 12
Australian Eastern Daylight Time (AEDT) – 4:00 March 12
New Zealand Daylight Time (NZDT) – 18:00 March 12

After 10 and a half years, do you still enjoy playing Rocket League, or is it dying? Let us know your opinions in the Insider Gaming Discord server

And to keep up with Rocket League updates as well as other esports and gaming news, sign up for the Insider Gaming newsletter

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android
